revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 永倉神社 |画像 = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]大字長坂字長坂36 | 緯度度 = 37 | 緯度分 = 8 | 緯度秒 = 51 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 11 | 経度秒 = 48 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[伊弉諾命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月16日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 祭神 == [[伊弉諾命]]を主祭神とし、[[熊野神|熊野三神]]を合祀する。 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[斉衡]]2年（[[855年]]）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）、それ以前の創建とされる。[[延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 == 境内 == 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 ファイル:Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|250px|本殿 ファイル:Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|250px|参道と階段 ファイル:Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|250px|稲荷神社 == 交通アクセス == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北本線]]「[[白河駅]]」から北西へ2km（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・[[東北新幹線]]「[[新白河駅]]」から北へ3km（徒歩約40分） == 関連項目 == *[[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:なかくらしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村]]

{{神社 |名称 = 永倉神社 |画像 = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]大字長坂字長坂36 | 緯度度 = 37 | 緯度分 = 8 | 緯度秒 = 51 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 11 | 経度秒 = 48 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[伊弉諾命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月16日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 祭神 == [[伊弉諾命]]を主祭神とし、[[熊野神|熊野三神]]を合祀する。 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[斉衡]]2年（[[855年]]）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）、それ以前の創建とされる。[[延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 == 境内 == 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 ファイル:Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|250px|本殿 ファイル:Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|250px|参道と階段 ファイル:Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|250px|稲荷神社 == 交通アクセス == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北本線]]「[[白河駅]]」から北西へ2km（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・[[東北新幹線]]「[[新白河駅]]」から北へ3km（徒歩約40分） == 参考文献 == *社頭案内板 == 関連項目 == *[[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:なかくらしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月16日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 祭神 == [[伊弉諾命]]を主祭神とし、[[熊野神|熊野三神]]を合祀する。 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[斉衡]]2年（[[855年]]）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）、それ以前の創建とされる。[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 == 境内 == 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|参道と階段 Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 == 交通アクセス == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北本線]]「[[白河駅]]」から北西へ2 [[キロメートル|km]]（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・[[東北新幹線]]「[[新白河駅]]」から北へ3 km（徒歩約40分） == 参考文献 == *社頭案内板 == 関連項目 == *[[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:なかくらしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{nihongo|'''Nagakura Shrine Haiden'''|永倉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagakura Shrine Haiden'''|永倉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagakura Shrine Haiden | native_name = 永倉神社 | image = Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Nagakura Shrine Haiden | coordinates = {{coord|37.147761|140.196576}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Nagakura Shrine Haiden'''|永倉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagakura Shrine Haiden'''|永倉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagakura Shrine Haiden'''|永倉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagakura Shrine Haiden | native_name = 永倉神社 | image = Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Nagakura Shrine Haiden | coordinates = {{coord|37.147761|140.196576}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Nagakura Shrine Haiden'''|永倉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2025年11月}} {{神社 |名称 = 永倉神社 |画像 = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]大字長坂字長坂36 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊弉諾命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月16日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=永倉神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 祭神 == [[伊弉諾命]]を主祭神とし、[[熊野神|熊野三神]]を合祀する。 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[斉衡]]2年（[[855年]]）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）、それ以前の創建とされる。[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 == 境内 == 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|参道と階段 Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 == 交通アクセス == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北本線]]「[[白河駅]]」から北西へ2 [[キロメートル|km]]（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・[[東北新幹線]]「[[新白河駅]]」から北へ3 km（徒歩約40分） == 参考文献 == *社頭案内板 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:なかくらしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{出典の明記|date=2025年11月}} {{神社 |名称 = 永倉神社 |画像 = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]大字長坂字長坂36 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊弉諾命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月16日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=永倉神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 祭神 == [[伊弉諾命]]を主祭神とし、[[熊野神|熊野三神]]を合祀する。 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[斉衡]]2年（[[855年]]）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）、それ以前の創建とされる。[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 == 境内 == 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|参道と階段 Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 == 交通アクセス == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北本線]]「[[白河駅]]」から北西へ2 [[キロメートル|km]]（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・[[東北新幹線]]「[[新白河駅]]」から北へ3 km（徒歩約40分） == 参考文献 == *社頭案内板 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:なかくらしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[JA:永倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282584}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|永倉神社|version=107319638|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2025年11月}} {{神社 |名称 = 永倉神社 |画像 = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]大字長坂字長坂36 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊弉諾命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月16日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=永倉神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[西白河郡]][[西郷村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 祭神 == [[伊弉諾命]]を主祭神とし、[[熊野神|熊野三神]]を合祀する。 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[斉衡]]2年（[[855年]]）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）、それ以前の創建とされる。[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 == 境内 == 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|参道と階段 Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 == 交通アクセス == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北本線]]「[[白河駅]]」から北西へ2 [[キロメートル|km]]（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・[[東北新幹線]]「[[新白河駅]]」から北へ3 km（徒歩約40分） == 参考文献 == *社頭案内板 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

旧{{jalink|村社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|神明造|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月16日|Q2897}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=永倉神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|西白河郡|Q1155789}}{{jalink|西郷村|Q766027}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は村社。 == 祭神 == {{jalink|伊弉諾命}}を主祭神とし、{{jalink|熊野神||熊野三神}}を合祀する。 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。{{jalink|斉衡|Q1324664}}2年（{{jalink|855年|Q25073}}）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』）、それ以前の創建とされる。{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）に編纂された{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}2年（{{jalink|1869年|Q7720}}）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 == 境内 == 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}9年（{{jalink|1997年|Q2088}}）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|参道と階段 Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 == 交通アクセス == *{{jalink|東日本旅客鉄道|Q499071|JR東日本}}{{jalink|東北本線|Q681190}}「{{jalink|白河駅|Q872774}}」から北西へ2 {{jalink|キロメートル|Q828224|km}}（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・{{jalink|東北新幹線|Q900665}}「{{jalink|新白河駅|Q1136381}}」から北へ3 km（徒歩約40分） == 参考文献 == *社頭案内板 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *{{jalink|陸奥国の式内社一覧|Q11658590}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|神明造|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月16日|Q2897}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=永倉神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|西白河郡|Q1155789}}{{jalink|西郷村|Q766027}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は村社。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|伊弉諾命}}を主祭神とし、{{jalink|熊野神||熊野三神}}を合祀する。 === 歴史 === 創建年代は不詳。{{jalink|斉衡|Q1324664}}2年（{{jalink|855年|Q25073}}）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』）、それ以前の創建とされる。{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）に編纂された{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}2年（{{jalink|1869年|Q7720}}）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 === 境内 === 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}9年（{{jalink|1997年|Q2088}}）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|参道と階段 Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 === 交通アクセス === *{{jalink|東日本旅客鉄道|Q499071|JR東日本}}{{jalink|東北本線|Q681190}}「{{jalink|白河駅|Q872774}}」から北西へ2 {{jalink|キロメートル|Q828224|km}}（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・{{jalink|東北新幹線|Q900665}}「{{jalink|新白河駅|Q1136381}}」から北へ3 km（徒歩約40分） === 参考文献 === *社頭案内板 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|陸奥国の式内社一覧|Q11658590}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:なかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2025年11月}} {{神社 |名称 = 永倉神社 |画像 = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|西白河郡|Q1155789}}{{jalink|西郷村|Q766027}}大字長坂字長坂36 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|伊弉諾命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|神明造|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月16日|Q2897}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=永倉神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''永倉神社'''（ながくらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|西白河郡|Q1155789}}{{jalink|西郷村|Q766027}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は村社。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|伊弉諾命}}を主祭神とし、{{jalink|熊野神||熊野三神}}を合祀する。 === 歴史 === 創建年代は不詳。{{jalink|斉衡|Q1324664}}2年（{{jalink|855年|Q25073}}）2月に官社に列したと記載があるため（『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』）、それ以前の創建とされる。{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）に編纂された{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「陸奥國白河郡 永倉神社」と記載があり、小社に列している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}2年（{{jalink|1869年|Q7720}}）山火事により社殿や社宝が焼失した。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）神明宮から永倉神社に復号された。 === 境内 === 拝殿及び本殿は明治2年（1869年）の火災後の再建で、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}9年（{{jalink|1997年|Q2088}}）に西郷村指定文化財に指定された。 Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|参道と階段 Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 === 交通アクセス === *{{jalink|東日本旅客鉄道|Q499071|JR東日本}}{{jalink|東北本線|Q681190}}「{{jalink|白河駅|Q872774}}」から北西へ2 {{jalink|キロメートル|Q828224|km}}（徒歩約30分） *JR東日本東北本線・{{jalink|東北新幹線|Q900665}}「{{jalink|新白河駅|Q1136381}}」から北へ3 km（徒歩約40分） === 参考文献 === *社頭案内板 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|陸奥国の式内社一覧|Q11658590}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:なかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=November 2025}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagakura Shrine |Image = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=November 2025}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagakura Shrine |Image = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets v destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:永倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282584}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=November 2025}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagakura Shrine |Image = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Haiden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182043 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:永倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282584}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=November 2025}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagakura Shrine |Image = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Haiden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182043 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:永倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282584}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=November 2025}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagakura Shrine |Image = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

Haiden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182043 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:永倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282584}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=November 2025}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagakura Shrine |Image = [[Image:Nagakura Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukushima Prefecture]]

Haiden (worship hall) |Location = 36 Nagasaka, Ōaza Nagasaka, Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (minor)
Former village shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Nagakura Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nagakura Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nagakura Shrine''' (ながくらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Nishigō Village, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture. It is a minor shrine listed in the Engishiki, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deity is Izanagi-no-Mikoto, with the Kumano deities enshrined jointly. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Since there is a record stating that it was listed as an official shrine in February of the 2nd year of Saikō (855) (in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*), it is considered to have been founded prior to that time. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, compiled in the 5th year of Engō (927), it is recorded as “Nagakura Shrine, Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province,” and is listed as a minor shrine. In the 2nd year of Meiji (1869), the shrine buildings and sacred treasures were destroyed by a mountain fire. In the 5th year of Meiji (1872), the shrine was renamed from Shinmei Shrine back to Nagakura Shrine. === Shrine grounds === The haiden and honden were rebuilt after the fire of 1869, and in the 9th year of Heisei (1997) they were designated as cultural properties of Nishigō Village. Nagakura Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall (honden) Nagakura Jinja Sandoukaidan.jpg|Approach and stairs Nagakura Jinja Inari Jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * From “Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line: 2 km northwest (about 30 minutes on foot) * From “Shin-Shirakawa Station” on the JR East Tōhoku Main Line and Tōhoku Shinkansen: 3 km north (about 40 minutes on foot) === References === * Information board at the shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagakura Shrine (Nishigō-mura)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:西郷村の建築物]] [[Category:西郷村の歴史]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukushima Prefecture]]
